title: `git init` fails inside the sandbox -> {allow, ng} (Bash auto-allowed, OS blocks; git prints fatal:)

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool, then report its output verbatim (do not summarize, do not run anything else): git init newrepo 2>err.txt && echo INITOK > INITMARK.txt; echo "exit=$?"; cat err.txt
probes.result_text:
```
exit=128
fatal: cannot copy '/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/share/git-core/templates/hooks/commit-msg.sample' to '$REPO/cases/02-sandbox-bash/S8-sandbox-git-interop/a-git-init-in-sandbox/newrepo/.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample': Operation not permitted
```

hypothesis: `git init` inside the sandbox does not complete: the success marker is never written. The captured `fatal:` stderr shows what git could not do (the reason multi-repo-workspace open-task.sh runs init/clone in the prep phase OUTSIDE the sandbox).
